product_overview: Icebreaker.io is a real-time anonymous stranger-matching web app where two people are paired, exchange turn-based messages, and then privately choose whether to stay connected or vanish. The app uses a lightweight, no-auth flow with an ephemeral in-memory session lifecycle.
core_goals: Allow a visitor to pick a short alias and join a live queue to find an anonymous stranger.
Pair two users into a short turn-based conversation (three messages each) that enforces turn order.
Surface a private decision step after the exchange so users can mutually stay connected or vanish.
Provide simple, discoverable safety and philosophy guidance as static reference pages.

features.name: Username Entry
features.description: Visitor provides an alias (letters, numbers, spaces, underscores, max 20 chars) and requests to join the matchmaking queue.
user_flows: Navigate to / -> Enter an alias within allowed characters and length -> Submit alias to request a match -> See a reveal modal explaining the experience -> Confirm the modal to proceed -> Be navigated to /waiting
Navigate to / -> Enter an alias containing invalid characters or exceeding 20 characters -> Submit alias -> See a validation error describing allowed characters and max length

features.name: Online Count Display
features.description: Show the current approximate number of strangers online so visitors see activity before joining the queue.
user_flows: Navigate to / -> Observe a pulsing indicator and the live online stranger count updating in the landing UI

features.name: Matchmaking Queue (Waiting Room)
features.description: User waits in a lobby while the app matches them with another available visitor; they can cancel while waiting.
user_flows: Be navigated to /waiting -> Join the matchmaking queue and see an orbiting ring animation indicating waiting -> Observe the online stranger count in the top-right panel -> Choose to leave the lobby to cancel matchmaking -> Be navigated back to /
Be navigated to /waiting -> Join the matchmaking queue and see an orbiting ring animation -> Observe a connection-found state when paired -> Be placed into a live session

features.name: Turn-based Conversation Session (3 messages each)
features.description: Two matched users receive the same prompt and alternate sending up to three messages each; conversation ends when both complete their turns or if a participant disconnects.
user_flows: Be placed into a live session with a matched stranger -> Receive the shared prompt -> Send message 1 on your turn -> See a waiting-for-stranger state while the stranger replies -> Receive stranger's message 1 -> Send message 2 when it becomes your turn -> Receive stranger's message 2 -> Send message 3 when it becomes your turn -> Receive stranger's message 3 -> See the decision screen after all messages are exchanged
Be placed into a live session -> Attempt to send a message when it is not your turn -> See a validation/rejection indicating it's not your turn and remain in waiting state

features.name: Decision & Result Screen
features.description: After the conversation both users privately choose whether to stay connected; if both agree a shareable link is shown, otherwise the session ends with no trace.
user_flows: See the decision screen after the conversation -> Privately choose Stay Connected -> See confirmation that both parties stayed and a shareable link is presented -> Optionally copy the share link to clipboard -> Choose to match again to return to the landing page and clear the session
See the decision screen after the conversation -> Choose Vanish (or take no action within decision timeout) -> See the gone outcome and a clean goodbye with no shared link

features.name: Static Safety & Philosophy Pages
features.description: Accessible informational pages that explain safety guidance and the project's philosophy.
user_flows: Navigate to /safety -> Read static safety information
Navigate to /philosophy -> Read static philosophy/about information

routes.description: Landing page with username input and "Find a Stranger" CTA
Waiting room — emits join_queue via Socket.io, waits for match_found
MCQ session — 3 rounds of multiple choice questions answered turn-by-turn
Result page — shows "you matched" or "gone" based on game outcome

features.name: Username Entry
features.description: User enters an alias (letters, numbers, spaces, underscores, max 20 chars) and clicks "Find a Stranger"
user_interactions: Type username into alias input field
Click "Find a Stranger" button or press Enter
See validation error for invalid characters
See RevealModal popup after valid submission
Click "Enter the Void" in modal to proceed to /waiting

features.name: Online Count Display
features.description: Shows live count of strangers online via Socket.io queue_update event
user_interactions: See pulsing dot with online stranger count on landing page

features.name: Matchmaking Queue
features.description: User waits in queue; server pairs two users and emits match_found
user_interactions: See orbiting ring animation while waiting
See online stranger count in top-right panel
Click "Leave Lobby" to cancel and return to landing
See "connection found" state when matched before navigating to session

features.name: MCQ Session (3 Rounds)
features.description: Two matched users answer 3 multiple-choice questions; answers revealed after both respond; mismatch ends session
user_interactions: See current round indicator (1 of 3, 2 of 3, 3 of 3)
Read question text
Click one of the answer options
See "waiting for the stranger" state after submitting
See round result (matched / no match) with both answers revealed
Progress to next question if matched
Navigate to result page after all 3 rounds or a mismatch
See disconnected state if stranger leaves

features.name: Result / Outcome Screen
features.description: Shows final outcome — "you matched" with share link if both stay connected, or "gone" if either vanishes
user_interactions: See "you matched" heading with stranger username if connected
Toggle identity sharing on/off
Copy share link to clipboard
Click "match again" to return to landing and clear session
See "gone." screen if outcome is vanished

features.name: Navigation (Safety & Philosophy pages)
features.description: Static informational pages accessible from navbar
user_interactions: Click "Safety" link in navbar
Click "Philosophy" link in navbar

known_limitations.issue: Session and Result pages rely on sessionStorage for state; direct URL access without prior flow will render nothing
known_limitations.impact: Tests must follow the full user flow rather than directly navigating to session/result URLs

known_limitations.issue: Real-time matching requires two simultaneous browser sessions; single-user tests cannot reach the session phase without a second socket client
known_limitations.impact: End-to-end session tests require coordinating two browser tabs or simulated socket clients
